LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: CROSS-DEFENDANT ALL BAY CITIES CONSTRUCTION'S DEMURRER TO THE 5TH AND 8TH CAUSES OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND IN ACCORDANCE WITH ERLICH V. MENEZES (1999) 21 CAL. 4TH 543. THE DEMURRER TO THE 9TH CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED, 10 DAYS TO ANSWER. PARAGRAPH 104 ALLEGES THAT CROSS-DEFENDANT ENGAGED IN FRAUD IN PROCURING A SIGNATURE ON A DOCUMENT AND THIS WAS DONE TO CAUSE CROSS-COMPLAINANT EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. THIS IS A DIFFERENT ALLEGATION THAN THE CHARGING ALLEGATIONS IN THE 5TH AND 8TH CAUSES OF ACTION. THE DEMURRER IS TIMELY FOR CROSS-COMPLAINANT DID NOT SEEK A DEFAULT. THERE IS NO LEGAL AUTHORITY FOR THE AWARD OF COSTS AND ATTORNEYS' FEES IN CONNECTION WITH THE DEMURRER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-13-2008 Text Ruling

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-13-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Julia M. Adams of the Epstein Group, 863-5718, appearing for cross-complainant and responding party Nancy Mattingly

No other appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Cross-defendant ALL BAY CITIES CONSTRUCTION'S demurrer to the 5th and 8th causes of action is sustained without leave to amend in accordance with Erlich v. Menezes (1999) 21 Cal. 4th 543. The demurrer to the 9th cause of action is overruled, 10 days to answer. Paragraph 104 alleges that cross-defendant engaged in fraud in procuring a signature on a document and this was done to cause cross-complainant emotional distress. This is a different allegation than the charging allegations in the 5th and 8th causes of action. The demurrer is timely for cross-complainant did not seek a default. There is no legal authority for the award of costs and attorneys' fees in connection with the demurrer. Order signed in open Court. (302/PJM/AY) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600
